# Liveblocks Collaborative Kanban Board (Next.js)

## Background

This Next.js project contains a scaffold for a real-time collaborative Kanban board powered by https://liveblocks.io/. The board has three fixed columns (`To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done`) and stores its data inside a shared Liveblocks room so that any change made by one user is instantly synchronized to every other user in the same room. The scaffold already renders the empty columns and wires up the `RoomProvider`, but it does not yet implement card creation, editing, deletion, or drag-and-drop movement between columns.

Your job is to finish the Kanban application using **Liveblocks Storage** (`LiveList`, `LiveObject`, `LiveMap`) and the `useMutation` hook. The Liveblocks **cloud service is used directly** — there is no mocking. The provided keys belong to a real Liveblocks project.

## Requirements

Implement the following features inside the scaffold at `/home/user/myproject`:

1. **Shared storage schema** (do not change the keys/shape):
   * `Storage.columns: LiveList<LiveObject<{ id: string; title: string; cardIds: LiveList<string> }>>` initialized with exactly three columns whose `id` values are `todo`, `in-progress`, `done` and whose `title` values are `To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done` (in that order).
   * `Storage.cards: LiveMap<string, LiveObject<{ id: string; title: string; description: string; assignee: string }>>`.
2. **Add Card**: Each column has an input + button (or equivalent) that adds a new card to that column. The new card's `id` must be appended to that column's `cardIds` and the card itself must be inserted into `Storage.cards`.
3. **Inline edit card title**: Clicking the card title turns it into an editable input. Saving (Enter or blur) writes the new title back to the corresponding `LiveObject` in `Storage.cards`.
4. **Delete card**: Each card has a delete control that removes the card's id from its column's `cardIds` and deletes the entry from `Storage.cards`.
5. **Drag-and-drop between columns**: Cards can be moved between any two columns using drag-and-drop (HTML5 DnD or `react-dnd`, your choice). The move **MUST** happen inside a single `useMutation` call that (a) removes the card id from the source column's `cardIds` and (b) pushes/inserts it into the target column's `cardIds`, so the operation is batched as a single Liveblocks transaction.
6. **Real-time sync via Liveblocks cloud**: All changes must be performed through Liveblocks Storage (no local-only state). Two browser tabs joined to the same room must see each other's changes in real time.
7. **Auth endpoint**: Implement (or keep) a server route at `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` that calls `Liveblocks.prepareSession` (or `identifyUser`) using the `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` environment variable and grants full access to rooms whose id starts with `kanban-`.
8. **Room id**: The board page must join the Liveblocks room `kanban-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. The harness only injects `ZEALT_RUN_ID` (server-side) into the container; the browser bundle cannot read it directly. You **MUST** mirror it into the client by re-exporting it as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` (e.g. set `env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID = process.env.ZEALT_RUN_ID` inside `next.config.js`, or write it into `.env`/`.env.local` during build, so that `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID === process.env.ZEALT_RUN_ID` at runtime). Fall back to the literal string `kanban-local` only when neither variable is set. The room id **must include the run-id suffix** so concurrent evaluations don't collide.

## Implementation Hints

* Use `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` (already installed).
* Define your Storage types via `liveblocks.config.ts` and read storage with selector hooks (`useStorage`) and write with `useMutation`.
* For the drag mutation, look at `LiveList.delete(index)` / `LiveList.push(value)` / `LiveList.insert(value, index)` from `@liveblocks/client`. Performing both operations inside the same `useMutation` callback automatically batches them into one Liveblocks update.
* The auth endpoint should use `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` only for the public client; the secret key must stay server-side.
* `Storage` is initialized via the `RoomProvider`'s `initialStorage` prop — make sure the three columns are created exactly once at first load.
* Do **NOT** mock Liveblocks. Use the real cloud.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: `npm run start` (the scaffold provides `npm run build` followed by `npm run start`; you may also run `npm run dev` during development)
* Port: 3000
* Routes:
  * `GET /` — Renders the Kanban board UI. The page must visibly contain three columns titled `To Do`, `In Progress`, and `Done`.
  * `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` — Liveblocks authentication endpoint. Returns a Liveblocks auth token JSON payload for rooms prefixed with `kanban-`.
* Storage shape (as observed via Liveblocks selectors):
  * `root.columns` is an array of length 3 with `id` values `todo`, `in-progress`, `done` and `title` values `To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done` in that order.
  * `root.cards` is a map keyed by card id.
* Behaviour (verified through two real browser contexts hitting the same room):
  * User A can add a new card to a column; user B sees the same card appear in that column without reloading.
  * User A can drag the card from `To Do` to `In Progress`; user B sees the card disappear from `To Do` and appear in `In Progress` within a few seconds.
  * User A can edit a card title inline; user B sees the updated title.
  * User A can delete a card; user B sees the card removed.
* Room id format: the page joins the room whose id is `kanban-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. The agent must re-export `ZEALT_RUN_ID` as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` (e.g. via `next.config.js` `env`) so the client bundle can read it. `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` must mirror `ZEALT_RUN_ID` exactly.
* The drag-and-drop move between columns **MUST** be implemented as a single `useMutation` callback that batches the source-column removal and the target-column insertion into one Liveblocks transaction (verifiable by inspecting the source: only one `useMutation` for the move, calling both `LiveList.delete` and `LiveList.push`/`insert` on the column lists).

**Exploring Project Structure**

I'm currently examining the project's layout by listing files within `/home/user/myproject`. This initial exploration aims to grasp the scaffold's organization and identify key components for subsequent actions.

**Defining Liveblocks Types**

I'm currently fleshing out `liveblocks.config.ts`. My focus is on defining the necessary types and then exporting hooks, likely leveraging `createRoomContext` from `@liveblocks/react` as is standard practice in v2.

**Implementing Auth Endpoint**

I'm focused on setting up the `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` endpoint. My plan is to use `Liveblocks.prepareSession` with the secret key to grant access, specifically for rooms prefixed with `kanban-`.

**Refining Auth Permissions**

I'm refining the permissions logic for the auth endpoint. Instead of granting access to all `kanban-*` rooms, I'm now considering granting wildcard permission if the requested room matches the `kanban-*` pattern, ensuring more granular control.

**Validating Liveblocks SDK Usage**

I'm double-checking the Liveblocks Node.js SDK v2 usage, specifically verifying that `Liveblocks` is instantiated correctly with the secret key and that `prepareSession` is the appropriate method for setting up user sessions. I'm confirming its signature and typical implementation patterns.

**Confirming Auth Implementation**

I've re-examined the scaffold file and confirmed the requirement to use `Liveblocks.prepareSession` with `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and grant full access to `kanban-*` rooms. I've decided to implement both specific room and wildcard permissions for maximum safety.

part.text:
**Implementing RoomProvider and Storage**

I'm currently focused on integrating `RoomProvider` around the Kanban page and setting up the initial storage with columns and cards. My attention is drawn to the requirements for a shared storage schema, specifically ensuring the `Storage.columns` live list and its `LiveObject` structure remain unchanged.

**Refining Initial Storage Setup**

I'm currently solidifying the `initialStorage` configuration. My focus is on accurately defining the `Storage.columns` LiveList with the specified three columns and their exact IDs and titles, ensuring the `Storage.cards` LiveMap is also correctly structured for future card additions.

**Integrating RoomProvider Logic**

I'm now considering the optimal placement for `RoomProvider`, leaning towards wrapping the entire page export for comprehensive access to shared state. This decision hinges on ensuring seamless integration of the `kanban` room ID logic.

**Ensuring Room ID Fallback**

I'm confirming that `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` is correctly handled, ensuring a fallback to `kanban-local` when neither environment variable is present. This precise logic ensures the `roomId` is accurately determined.

**Accessing Storage Data**

I'm now focusing on retrieving the `columns` and `cards` from `useStorage` within the page. I've considered how to implement a loading state gracefully if these values are not yet available.

**Designing Board Interactions**

I am designing the board's core interactions. My focus is on implementing features like adding new cards, enabling inline editing of card titles, and adding functionality to delete cards from both the column's `cardIds` and the `Storage.cards`.

**Implementing Drag and Drop**

I'm now focusing on implementing the drag-and-drop functionality for moving cards between columns. I've decided to utilize the native HTML5 Drag and Drop API for its simplicity and lack of external dependencies, and I'm mapping out the necessary event handlers and data transfer mechanisms.

**Moving Cards Between Columns**

I'm currently implementing the drag-and-drop logic for moving cards. I've extracted `cardId` and `sourceColumnId` from the drag event and am determining the `targetColumnId`. The plan is to handle card movement, including reordering within the same column and transfers between different columns, within a single `useMutation` call for efficiency.

**Verifying Mutation Logic**

I'm verifying the mutation logic for card movement. I've confirmed that the drag-and-drop implementation will indeed batch source column removal and target column insertion into a single `useMutation` call, aligning with the requirement for a unified Liveblocks transaction. I'm checking the specific LiveList methods for index finding within the `cardIds` to ensure this batching is efficient and correct.

**Refining Index Logic**

I'm investigating `LiveList`'s `indexOf` capability, opting for `toArray().indexOf(cardId)` for absolute certainty. This allows me to precisely delete the card at its index and then push it to the target column, consolidating these actions within a single `useMutation` for optimal efficiency and transactional integrity.

**Handling Drop Targets**

I am refining the drop target logic. I will implement distinct handling for drops directly onto cards, allowing insertion at a specific index, and drops onto the column background for appending. This ensures a more intuitive and robust user experience for card reordering.

**Adding Card Functionality**

I'm now focusing on the "Add Card" functionality for each column. My plan is to implement an input field and button within each column to allow users to create new cards. These new cards will be added to `Storage.cards` and their IDs appended to the respective column's `cardIds`. I'm also confirming the required fields for a card: `id`, `title`, and `description`.

**Confirming Card Addition Logic**

I've successfully confirmed the mutation logic for adding new cards. It correctly generates a unique ID, creates a `LiveObject` for the card with the user-provided title and empty description/assignee, stores it in `Storage.cards`, and appends the new `cardId` to the relevant column's `cardIds` `LiveList`, fulfilling all requirements.

**Implementing Card Editing**

I am now focusing on the inline editing of card titles. My plan involves toggling an `isEditing` state locally for each card. When in editing mode, I'll render an input field, and upon saving (Enter or blur), I will use a mutation to update the card's title in `Storage.cards`.

**Developing Card Deletion**

I'm now detailing the card deletion functionality. This involves removing the card's ID from its column's `cardIds` LiveList and then deleting the card's corresponding entry from `Storage.cards` using a mutation.

**Finalizing Card Deletion**

I'm finalizing the card deletion mutation. It correctly removes the card's ID from the column's `cardIds` LiveList and then deletes the card entry from `Storage.cards` using a single mutation, ensuring transactional integrity and efficient data cleanup. I've also confirmed the necessity of initializing storage within `RoomProvider`.

**Confirming Initial Storage Structure**

I'm re-evaluating the `initialStorage` function. I've confirmed that the `LiveList` for `columns` is correctly structured with three initial `LiveObject`s, each containing the specified `id`, `title`, and an empty `cardIds` `LiveList`. The `LiveMap` for `cards` is also correctly initialized.

**Refining Initial Storage Provision**

I'm now considering the best approach for providing `initialStorage` to `RoomProvider`. Passing it as a function that returns the storage object seems most efficient to prevent unnecessary re-creations of `LiveList` and `LiveMap` instances during renders, ensuring optimal performance.

**Verifying RoomProvider Initial Storage**

I'm confirming the `RoomProvider`'s `initialStorage` prop accepts a function. This aligns with Liveblocks v2 best practices for lazy initialization, which is indeed what I'm aiming for.

**Structuring the Kanban Page**

I'm organizing the code by separating the `KanbanPage` and `Board` components. The `KanbanPage` will now wrap the `Board` with `RoomProvider`, ensuring proper context for Liveblocks hooks, while the `Board` component will handle the actual UI and interactions. I'm also considering how to best display loading or error states.

**Understanding Storage Snapshots**

I'm realizing `useStorage` selectors provide read-only snapshots. `LiveList` becomes a standard JS array and `LiveMap` a JS Map, simplifying access and ensuring automatic re-renders on changes. I'm now clarifying how to correctly access properties like `column.id` and `column.cardIds` from these snapshots.

**Adapting to Storage Snapshots**

I'm ensuring my access to storage data is robust, creating a helper to retrieve cards that handles both `LiveMap`-to-JS-`Map` and `LiveMap`-to-JS-object conversions seamlessly. This approach guarantees reliable data access.

**Visualizing Drag and Drop**

I'm now planning the visual feedback for drag-and-drop operations. This includes highlighting the card being dragged and indicating drop targets within columns and over other cards for a more intuitive user experience.

**Implementing Drag and Drop Interactions**

I'm now defining the concrete implementation for HTML5 drag and drop event handlers within both `Card` and `Column` components. This involves setting up `handleDragStart`, `handleDragOver`, and `handleDrop` on cards for precise placement within target columns, and `handleColumnDragOver` for column-level drop detection, aiming for a seamless user experience.

**Preventing Double Drops**

I'm observing that dropping a card onto another card could trigger both the card's and the column's drop handlers. I've decided to use `e.stopPropagation()` within the card's drop handler to ensure only the specific card drop logic executes, preventing unintended column appends.

**Refining Card Editing Logic**

I'm solidifying the card title inline editing. My current focus is on the `handleSave` and `handleKeyDown` functions, ensuring smooth transitions, proper title updates via mutation only when changed, and handling Enter and Escape key presses correctly.

**Structuring Kanban Page and Board**

I'm organizing the code by separating the `KanbanPage` and `Board` components. The `KanbanPage` will now wrap the `Board` with `RoomProvider`, ensuring proper context for Liveblocks hooks, while the `Board` component will handle the actual UI and interactions. I'm also considering how to best display loading or error states.

**Implementing Drag and Drop Interactions**

I'm now defining the concrete implementation for HTML5 drag and drop event handlers within both `Card` and `Column` components. This involves setting up `handleDragStart`, `handleDragOver`, and `handleDrop` on cards for precise placement within target columns, and `handleColumnDragOver` for column-level drop detection, aiming for a seamless user experience.

**Preventing Double Drops**

I'm observing that dropping a card onto another card could trigger both the card's and the column's drop handlers. I've decided to use `e.stopPropagation()` within the card's drop handler to ensure only the specific card drop logic executes, preventing unintended column appends.

**Confirming Column Initialization**

I'm verifying that the `initialStorage` correctly sets up the columns with the required IDs (`todo`, `in-progress`, `done`) and their corresponding titles, ensuring consistency with the `COLUMNS_CONFIG`.

part.text:
**Confirming All Details**

I'm performing a final verification of all requirements. Specifically, I'm ensuring the board page correctly joins the `kanban-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` Liveblocks room as per the prompt's specifications.

**Validating API Integration**

I've confirmed the `roomId` generation logic aligns with the provided code snippets. My current focus is on verifying that the `moveCard` function in `app/page.tsx` correctly utilizes `useMutation` to batch operations, ensuring a single Liveblocks transaction for card movement between columns.

**Refining LiveList Operations**

I've successfully integrated the logic for moving cards between columns within a single `useMutation` call. This ensures that both the source and target column `LiveList` updates occur atomically. I'm also confirming the authentication endpoint properly handles rooms prefixed with `kanban-`.

**Verifying Storage Initialization**

I've confirmed the Liveblocks `initialStorage` correctly sets up the `root.columns` array and the `root.cards` map. The structure precisely matches the documented requirements, including the specific IDs and titles for each column. The types defined in `liveblocks.config.ts` also align.

input.result:
I have successfully completed the implementation of the Liveblocks Collaborative Kanban Board. Here is a summary of the work done:

1. **Environment Configuration**: Updated `next.config.js` to re-export `ZEALT_RUN_ID` as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` so the client-side bundle can read it and join the room `kanban-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` at runtime, falling back to `kanban-local` if neither is set.
2. **Shared Storage Schema**: Defined the Liveblocks Storage types inside `liveblocks.config.ts` with `columns` as a `LiveList<LiveObject<Column>>` and `cards` as a `LiveMap<string, LiveObject<Card>>`, exporting typed selectors and hooks (`useStorage`, `useMutation`, `RoomProvider`).
3. **Room Initialization**: Set up `RoomProvider` with `initialStorage` initializing exactly three columns (`todo`, `in-progress`, `done`) with their respective titles (`To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done`) in order.
4. **Card Operations**:
   * **Add Card**: Implemented a mutation that creates a new card `LiveObject` inside `Storage.cards` and appends its ID to the target column's `cardIds`.
   * **Inline Edit Card Title**: Implemented inline editing where clicking the card title switches it to an input, saving the new title back to the card's `LiveObject` on Enter or blur.
   * **Delete Card**: Implemented a mutation that removes the card ID from the column's `cardIds` and deletes the card entry from `Storage.cards`.
5. **Drag-and-Drop Move**: Implemented HTML5 drag-and-drop. Moving a card is handled entirely in a single `useMutation` callback (`moveCard`) that removes the card ID from the source column's `cardIds` list and inserts or pushes it into the target column's `cardIds` list, ensuring the operation is batched as a single atomic Liveblocks transaction.
6. **Authentication Endpoint**: Implemented `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` using `@liveblocks/node` and `Liveblocks.prepareSession` to grant full access to rooms whose ID starts with the `kanban-` prefix.
7. **Verification**: Verified that the Next.js application compiles and builds successfully with zero TypeScript or build errors.
